model_name: MiLMMT-46-4B-v0.1 Q6_K GGUF (mradermacher quant of xiaomi-research/MiLMMT-46-4B-v0.1)
runtime_conversion_notes: Single-file GGUF (MiLMMT-46-4B-v0.1.Q6_K.gguf, community Q6_K static quant by mradermacher of xiaomi-research/MiLMMT-46-4B-v0.1). MiLMMT-46-4B is a Gemma-3-4B fine-tune for many-to-many MT, so its GGUF uses the gemma3 architecture. Run natively by llama.cpp (no weight-format conversion performed by flex-translate). The app builds llama.cpp from a vendored, pinned source tree (commit de6f727, CPU backend only) via Android NDK CMake, producing libllama.so + libggml*.so (arm64-v8a) plus a thin JNI shim libmilmmt_jni.so (apps/android/app/src/main/cpp/). This llama.cpp libllama.so is SEPARATE from the sherpa ASR runtime (libsherpaort13.so) and the Microsoft ONNX Runtime (libonnxruntime.so) used by the M2M-100 balanced MT path — distinct SONAMEs, all three coexist in one process. Greedy decode (top_k=1/temp=0 per the model card) over the completion-style MT prompt 'Translate this from <Source> to <Target>:\n<Source>: <text>\n<Target>:' with full English language names (Russian/English/Chinese (Simplified)).
notes: WS4 QUALITY on-device MT tier — user-selectable alongside M2M-100 (balanced) and Gemini Flash (cloud). MiLMMT-46-4B (Xiaomi Inc.) is a fine-tune of google/gemma-3-4b-pt for 46-language many-to-many translation (incl. RU/EN/ZH), released under the Gemma license. The owner ACCEPTED the Gemma Terms of Use and the Gemma Prohibited Use Policy for distribution on 2026-06-01. The license requires PASS-THROUGH: the APP must surface the Gemma Terms + Prohibited Use Policy to end users when MiLMMT is selected/downloaded — implemented as a license disclosure (notice text + tappable terms link) in the Модели screen for the MiLMMT pack (license_url=https://ai.google.dev/gemma/terms). Weights are downloaded to the device (first_run_download, ~3.74 GB / 3568.0542 MB), NOT bundled in the APK. A2 demo evidence is NOT a launch-support claim; the support matrix still requires WS6 benchmark evidence. Latency note: a 4B LLM on mobile is slow (seconds per translation), acceptable for the quality tier and a WS6 benchmark/gate concern, not a correctness one.
